apixaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition increases apixaban AUC ~100%

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition increases rivaroxaban exposure

dabigatran.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases dabigatran exposure (no CYP3A4 component)
dabigatran.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

edoxaban.mechanism: P-gp inhibition; edoxaban has lowest CYP3A4 dependence
edoxaban.recommendation: Lowest interaction risk among DOACs. Monitor.

notes: Ibrutinib itself increases bleeding risk via BTK-mediated platelet inhibition.

apixaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases apixaban exposure (bleeding risk)

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases rivaroxaban exposure (bleeding risk)
rivaroxaban.recommendation: AVOID or dose-reduce. Use LMWH instead.

dabigatran.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases dabigatran exposure; no CYP3A4 component
dabigatran.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

edoxaban.mechanism: P-gp inhibition modestly increases edoxaban; minimal CYP3A4 dependence
edoxaban.recommendation: Lowest interaction risk among DOACs. Monitor.

notes: P-gp inhibition demonstrated in vitro; treat as strong inhibitor for DOAC safety.

apixaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases apixaban exposure
apixaban.recommendation: AVOID, or reduce apixaban dose by 50% per FDA labeling. Prefer LMWH.

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases rivaroxaban exposure

dabigatran.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases dabigatran exposure; no CYP3A4 component
dabigatran.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

edoxaban.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases edoxaban exposure
edoxaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

notes: Commonly co-prescribed antifungal in oncology; one of the most significant CYP3A4/P-gp inhibitors.

apixaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases apixaban exposure
apixaban.recommendation: AVOID, or reduce apixaban dose by 50% per FDA labeling. Prefer LMWH.

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases rivaroxaban exposure
rivaroxaban.recommendation: CONTRAINDICATED with rivaroxaban (FDA labeling). Use LMWH instead.

dabigatran.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases dabigatran exposure; no CYP3A4 component
dabigatran.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

edoxaban.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases edoxaban exposure
edoxaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

notes: Rivaroxaban is contraindicated with ketoconazole per FDA labeling.

apixaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases apixaban exposure

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong dual CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibition markedly increases rivaroxaban exposure

dabigatran.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases dabigatran exposure; no CYP3A4 component
dabigatran.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

edoxaban.mechanism: P-gp inhibition increases edoxaban exposure
edoxaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding.

notes: Frequently used for antifungal prophylaxis in hematologic malignancy.

apixaban.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 inhibition with moderate P-gp inhibition increases apixaban exposure
apixaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding; consider LMWH.

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 inhibition increases rivaroxaban exposure
rivaroxaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for bleeding; consider LMWH.

dabigatran.mechanism: Moderate P-gp inhibition; dabigatran has no CYP3A4 dependence

edoxaban.mechanism: Moderate P-gp inhibition; edoxaban minimally CYP3A4 dependent

notes: Less P-gp effect than itraconazole/ketoconazole, but a strong CYP3A4 inhibitor.

apixaban.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 induction (with P-gp induction) lowers apixaban levels, risking reduced efficacy/thrombosis

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 induction (with P-gp induction) lowers rivaroxaban levels, risking reduced efficacy/thrombosis

dabigatran.mechanism: P-gp induction may modestly lower dabigatran levels; no CYP3A4 dependence

edoxaban.mechanism: Minimal CYP3A4 dependence; limited effect on edoxaban

notes: Inducer interaction reduces DOAC efficacy (thrombosis risk) — the danger is under-anticoagulation, not bleeding.

apixaban.mechanism: Strong P-gp induction may lower apixaban exposure, reducing anticoagulant efficacy
apixaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for reduced efficacy (breakthrough VTE); consider LMWH.

rivaroxaban.mechanism: Strong P-gp induction may lower rivaroxaban exposure, reducing anticoagulant efficacy
rivaroxaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for reduced efficacy (breakthrough VTE); consider LMWH.

dabigatran.mechanism: Strong P-gp induction may lower dabigatran exposure (P-gp substrate), reducing efficacy
dabigatran.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for reduced efficacy (breakthrough VTE).

edoxaban.mechanism: Strong P-gp induction may lower edoxaban exposure (P-gp substrate), reducing efficacy
edoxaban.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for reduced efficacy (breakthrough VTE).

notes: Strong P-gp inducer (plan Part 3C): may reduce DOAC levels and anticoagulant efficacy across all DOACs.
